If the true track is 270° and variation is 15°E the magnetic track is

children.text: An aircraft travels 30 NM of a 90 NM trip when the pilot discovers that the aircraft is 2.5 NM to the left of the required track. Using the 1 in 60 rule the aircraft is
To find whether an airport has VHF/DF (VDF) capability
During a long cross country flight a diversion to an airport that is long distance away becomes necessary. The proper course of action is to

answers: break up the diversion into shorter more manageable legs.
set up for a precautionary landing in a close by field and declare PAN PAN PAN PAN PAN PAN.
divert to a closer airport regardless of the condition of the airport.
use flight planning tools to form a detailed plan in the cockpit.
correctAnswer: break up the diversion into shorter more manageable legs.

children.text: Isogonic lines on maps
Dead reckoning is when navigating without

answers: the use of a navigation log.
the use of track lines on the map.
correctAnswer: the use of track lines on the map.

In the Northern Hemisphere a magnetic compass will normally indicate initially a turn toward the west

answers: when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.
during deceleration while on a southerly heading.
during acceleration while on a northerly heading.
correctAnswer: when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.

children.text: In the Northern Hemisphere a magnetic compass will normally indicate initially a turn toward the east

answers: during acceleration while on a northerly heading.
during deceleration while on a southerly heading.
when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
when entering a right turn from a northerly heading.
correctAnswer: when entering a left turn from a northerly heading.
